food_name: Kopi-C (Singapore Robusta coffee with evaporated milk and sugar, hot)

local_names.hindi: कोपी-सी (वाष्पीकृत दूध के साथ सिंगापुर कॉफ़ी)

aliases: कोपी-सी (वाष्पीकृत दूध के साथ सिंगापुर कॉफ़ी)

hindi: कोपी-சी (वाष्पीकृत दूध के साथ सिंगापुर कॉफ़ी)

food_subgroup: Hot coffee beverage — Robusta coffee concentrate with unsweetened evaporated milk (~8% fat) and white sugar added separately

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic coffee beverage category (FOODON_03306174) but no specific entry for Kopi-C (Singapore Robusta coffee with evaporated milk and sugar). Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coffee beverage — used as closest parent for Kopi-C (Singapore kopitiam Robusta coffee with evaporated milk and sugar, hot)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to Kopi-C exists. A0KZJ (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent. Kopi-C identity documented in description.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Kopi-C or Singapore kopitiam coffee beverages. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard kopitiam cup of Kopi-C is approximately 220 ml (220 g), the conventional serving size used in Singapore coffee shops and hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: Kopi-C (evaporated milk + sugar coffee) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists Kopi-C as a named entry with per-100g nutrient values. Values are taken directly from this primary Singapore regional source. All per_100g values reflect the composite beverage as served (Robusta coffee concentrate + evaporated milk + sugar).

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index Database — coffee with milk and sugar analogous estimate; Kopi-C GI estimated at 65–75 (midpoint 68) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for coffee with milk and sugar
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Kopi-C in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 68 (medium) is estimated from analogous entries for coffee with milk and sugar in the Sydney GI Database. The evaporated milk fat content moderates the glycaemic response relative to black coffee with sugar alone. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 11.44) / 100 ≈ 7.8, where carbohydrate per 220g serving = 5.2 × 2.2 = 11.44g. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (22 mg/100g); caffeine load from Robusta coffee (~100–150 mg per cup) is the primary cardiovascular consideration
hypertension.guidance: Kopi-C is low in sodium (22 mg/100g; ~48 mg per 220g serving) and poses minimal dietary sodium risk for hypertensive patients, though the Robusta caffeine content (~100–150 mg per cup) should be monitored in caffeine-sensitive individuals with hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycaemic load per serving (GL ~7.8); sugar content (~8–12g per cup) is significantly lower than Kopi (condensed milk); evaporated milk fat moderates glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kopi-C has a moderate glycaemic load of approximately 7.8 per standard cup — substantially lower than Kopi (GL ~16.8) — making it a more suitable kopitiam coffee choice for patients with Type 2 Diabetes, though requesting 'siu dai' (less sugar) further reduces GL to approximately 4–5.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from evaporated milk (1.2g/100g; ~2.6g per serving); cholesterol 8mg/100g; lower sugar burden than Kopi reduces overall cardiometabolic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kopi-C contributes approximately 2.6g saturated fat per standard cup from evaporated milk, which is moderate; its significantly lower sugar content compared to Kopi (condensed milk) reduces overall cardiometabolic risk, and it is preferable to Kopi for cardiovascular disease patients who wish to retain a milky kopitiam coffee.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lower sugar than Kopi reduces pro-inflammatory glycaemic impact; coffee chlorogenic acids retained; calcium from evaporated milk supports bone health
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kopi-C's moderate sugar content (lower than Kopi) reduces pro-inflammatory glycaemic impact, and the evaporated milk provides calcium (38 mg/100g composite) relevant for bone health in rheumatoid arthritis patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kopi-C is a traditional Singapore kopitiam (coffee shop) beverage made from strong Robusta coffee concentrate brewed through a cloth sock filter, combined with unsweetened evaporated milk (approximately 8% fat, traditionally Carnation brand — hence the 'C') and white sugar added separately. It occupies the middle tier of Singapore's kopitiam coffee matrix: richer and creamier than Kopi-O (black coffee with sugar only), but significantly less sweet than Kopi (which uses pre-sweetened condensed milk). Because the evaporated milk is unsweetened, the sugar level is controlled independently, making Kopi-C naturally lower in sugar (~8–12g per cup) compared to Kopi (~15–22g per cup). Nutritionally per 220ml serving: approximately 84 kcal, 2.2g protein, 4.0g fat (2.6g saturated), 11.4g carbohydrate, 9.9g sugar, 48mg sodium. The glycaemic load per serving is approximately 7.8 (medium), making it a clinically preferred kopitiam coffee choice for patients with Type 2 Diabetes transitioning away from Kopi. The Robusta coffee base contributes chlorogenic acids with antioxidant properties, and the evaporated milk provides calcium (38mg/100g) and small amounts of vitamin A, B2, and B12. Kopi-C is widely available at all Singapore kopitiams, hawker centres, and coffee shops, and is a culturally embedded daily beverage for Singaporeans of Chinese, Malay, and Indian heritage alike.
meal_context: transitional kopitiam beverage for diabetes management

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved regional source for Singapore dishes. Kopi-C is a named entry in HPB Foodo. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (confidence 0.9) as no direct Kopi-C GI entry exists. Ontology uses closest-parent codes with honest gap notes. All mandatory multilingual names for a Singaporean dish are present.
notes: Dietary fibre is 0.0g — naturally zero as Kopi-C is a liquid beverage with no plant fibre components; confirmed zero for this beverage type. Trans fat is 0.0g — confirmed zero per HPB source. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values are not reported by HPB Foodo for this entry and are set to -1. Water content not reported by source; set to -1. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) — no direct GI entry for Kopi-C exists in the University of Sydney GI Database; estimated from coffee with milk and sugar analogues. The 'C' in Kopi-C stands for 'Carnation', the brand of evaporated milk traditionally used in Singapore kopitiams; the term has become generic for evaporated milk in this context. Portion sizes: standard kopitiam cup 220g (default); large cup 350g; standard cup 250g also documented. Only the 220g serving is used as the canonical default serving size in this record.

Full bidirectional consistency now confirmed.
